name: intake-scoping
description: Gather project requirements, define scope boundaries, identify stakeholders, and produce a scoping document with acceptance criteria
objectives: Extract clear requirements from stakeholder input (tickets, messages, briefs)
Identify key stakeholders and their roles (RACI)
Produce a structured scoping document with acceptance criteria
Flag ambiguities or missing information for follow-up
Estimate rough effort and complexity tier (S/M/L/XL)
prerequisites: Project or feature request exists with initial description
Access to project context (existing tasks, docs, history)

procedure.description: Parse stakeholder input to extract functional and non-functional requirements
commands: READ project brief, ticket, or conversation transcript
EXTRACT functional requirements as user stories
IDENTIFY non-functional requirements (performance, security, UX)
LIST assumptions made during extraction
FLAG ambiguous or contradictory requirements
procedure.expectedOutput: Structured list of requirements with assumptions and ambiguities

procedure.description: Define what is in scope and explicitly what is not
commands: CATEGORIZE requirements into must-have, should-have, nice-to-have (MoSCoW)
DEFINE explicit out-of-scope items
IDENTIFY dependencies on external systems or teams
MAP requirements to existing codebase modules
ESTIMATE complexity tier per requirement
procedure.expectedOutput: Scope document with MoSCoW prioritization and boundaries

procedure.description: Identify all stakeholders and their involvement level
commands: LIST all stakeholders (requestor, approver, implementers, testers)
ASSIGN RACI roles (Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed)
IDENTIFY communication preferences and cadence
NOTE decision-making authority for scope changes

procedure.description: Define measurable acceptance criteria for each requirement
commands: WRITE acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format
DEFINE definition of done for the overall project
IDENTIFY testing requirements per criterion
SET quality gates and review checkpoints
procedure.expectedOutput: Acceptance criteria document with definition of done

procedure.description: Compile all artifacts into a final scoping document
commands: COMPILE requirements, scope, RACI, and acceptance criteria
ADD rough timeline estimate and effort sizing
INCLUDE risk register with initial risks
CREATE follow-up questions list for stakeholders
GENERATE project kickoff checklist
procedure.expectedOutput: Complete scoping document ready for stakeholder review

criteria: All requirements have clear acceptance criteria
No ambiguous requirements remain without follow-up flags
Effort estimate is within reasonable range for complexity

testScenarios.test: Submit a vague feature request with missing details
testScenarios.expected: Agent flags ambiguities and generates clarifying questions

testScenarios.test: Include requirements that clearly exceed original brief

successIndicators: Scoping document produced within expected duration
Stakeholder sign-off obtained without major revisions
No requirements missed during implementation phase

commonErrors.solution: Generate targeted clarifying questions for stakeholder
Document conflicts, propose resolution options, escalate to decision-maker

commonErrors.error: Scope too large for single iteration
commonErrors.solution: Propose phased delivery with milestones per phase

rollbackProcedure: ARCHIVE incomplete scoping document as draft
escalationCriteria: Requirements conflict cannot be resolved without executive decision
Project scope exceeds team capacity by >200%
Critical dependencies on unavailable external systems

successMetrics.name: Requirement Completeness
successMetrics.measurement: Requirements identified at scoping vs discovered during implementation

successMetrics.name: Stakeholder Alignment
successMetrics.measurement: Number of revision cycles before sign-off

agentInterfaces.message: New project scoped — create project structure and initial tasks
Notify stakeholders of scoping completion and review request
